rationale: Risk Manager VETO forbids any add; existing SOL long is ~15% of equity, 3x the per-position cap, so I cannot enter and must reduce. I trim the SOL position back toward the 5% limit (close ~10% of equity worth) to cut the correlated long-beta stack while leaders (BTC 1m -0.749, ETH 4h short) wobble and 15m RSI 80.3 is overbought with 1m rolling over. This keeps directional exposure aligned with the constructive 4h read but respects the mandated 5% max SOL exposure rather than chasing into noise.

transcript.research: RESEARCH BRIEFING (decisions/_scan_latest.json, bybit-mainnet-live, fresh). State of universe: SOL/USDT @72.71 composite 89.3% long, ALIGNED, strong uptrend 5/6 TFs, 15m RSI 80.3 overbought, 4h neutral, 1m fading. XRP @1.0688 composite 77.0% long ALIGNED, near-perfect intraday stack (1h-5m all 1.0) but extremely overbought (15m RSI 84.0), 4h flat. ETH @1595.88 composite 58.4% NOT aligned, intraday strong but 4h short (RSI 37.4, MACD -24.8). BTC @60574.4 composite 46.5% NOT aligned, pinched between flat 4h and short 1m (-0.749). BNB @564.7 composite 27.1%, weak/flat higher TFs. Notes: broad lower-TF volatility compression; overbought clustering XRP/SOL 15m; 4h neutral-to-negative across the whole universe (common drag). Ranked shortlist: 1) SOL/USDT LONG - highest composite 89.3%, aligned, 1h-5m block 0.87-1.0; caveat 15m RSI 80.3 overbought, 1m cooling. 2) XRP/USDT LONG - cleanest intraday alignment (four TFs exactly 1.0) but most overbought, no HTF backing. 3) ETH/USDT LONG (low conviction) - aligned:false, 4h outright short. BTC and BNB no clear edge. Flags: stretched/overbought XRP & SOL 15m; volatility coil universe-wide; timeframe conflict on ETH/BTC; all bullish reads rely on 1h-15m while every 4h is neutral/negative.
transcript.bull: BULL CASE - SOL/USDT LONG. SOL is the cleanest multi-TF alignment in the universe AND the one name whose 4h is not fighting it (4h score +0.495, RSI 59.5, MACD +0.625) - unlike XRP (4h -0.054) and ETH (4h outright short, MACD -24.8). Confluence stack: 1h 0.937 RSI 68.7 MACD +0.55, 30m 0.872 RSI 62.2, 5m 0.942 RSI 69.2, composite 89.3% aligned:true. Removes the biggest tail risk (HTF reversal). Honest caveats: 15m RSI 80.3 overbought (but least of the three; persistent overbought on trend = strength, argues for shallow-pullback entry); 1m cooled to 0.35 with MACD -0.004 (a micro-pause, treated as a gift for entry timing not a reversal). TRADE: LONG, entry 72.20-72.55 (let cooling 1m pull it back; don't chase 72.71+), stop 71.55 (below 30m structure, ~2x 1h ATR), T1 73.85 (~1.7:1), T2 74.60 (~2.6:1). Scale half at T1, trail rest. Size: standard 1R on the pullback, not a max chase - discipline is in entry price not size. Secondary: XRP tempting (5 TFs at 1.0) but blow-off profile with no HTF backstop; ETH a pass (4h short = knife). Bottom line: SOL long is the only candidate with full top-down agreement including a constructive 4h.
transcript.bear: BEAR REBUTTAL - STAY OUT (do not short). 1) The '4h leaning with the trade' claim is the weakest leg: 4h trend reads 'neutral', RSI 59.5, score 0.495 barely above midpoint - the safety anchor is NOT an established uptrend; conviction lives entirely in the overbought 1h-5m band. 2) This IS a chase dressed as a pullback: 15m RSI 80.3 pinned at 1.0, entry 72.20-72.55 only ~0.2-0.7% below last 72.71 - buying the top of the candle. A real reset would take price well below 72. 3) The 1m isn't 'cooling', it's rolling over (score 0.35, RSI flat 50, MACD negative -0.004) - momentum turns on the fast TF first; the bull treats the one honest leg as noise. 4) Stop inside the noise band: entry~72.38 to 71.55 = ~$0.83 (1.15%) but 1h ATR ~$0.52 - an ordinary 2-bar pullback to reset 15m RSI clips it. Worst outcome: right on direction, stopped on volatility; the 1.7:1 R:R is illusory because the stop is mispriced vs ATR. 5) Sector tape is a warning: 4h dead/negative across the complex (XRP -0.054, ETH -0.126 short, BTC -0.086), BTC 1m flipped -0.749. Leaders not confirming an isolated alt long into 15m RSI 80. Recommendation: decline the long as specified - entry has no edge. If forced, demand a real reset: wait for 15m RSI to low-60s and 1m MACD positive, enter the 71.2-71.6 shelf with stop below ~70.8 (outside 1h ATR).
transcript.risk: RISK MANAGER - VETO. Critical fact: we ALREADY HOLD a SOL/USDT long (20.6 SOL @ 72.60, mark 72.85, notional ~$1,500 = ~15% of equity), already 3x the configured 5% per-position cap. Account: equity ~$9,996, available $8,509, drawdown 0.07% (far from 20% limit). Open positions: BTC + XRP + SOL, all long-beta, all 3x. Config: position_size_pct 5%, leverage 3x, stop_loss_pct 2%, max_drawdown_pct 20%. (1) Adding a 1R (~$1,500) SOL long would push SOL to ~30% of equity = 6x breach of the 5% cap; config permits only ~$500 new notional. (2) Liquidation (~-33%, ~$48) is not binding; the 71.55 stop is the real exit - fine structurally. (3) Drawdown not the constraint - concentration is. (4) Stop integrity: bear is correct - ~$0.83 stop vs 1h ATR $0.52, with 15m RSI 80.3 and 1m MACD negative, a routine pullback clips it; chasing at 72.71 worsens it. Correlation: BTC 1m -0.749, ETH 4h short - leaders not confirming; adding SOL stacks correlated long-beta. VERDICT: VETO on the proposed add. Max add-on to existing SOL = 0%. Only as a fresh position (after closing existing) would 5% (~$500 notional at 3x) be allowed. Total SOL exposure must never exceed 5% of equity. Conditional: no pyramiding; if fresh, widen stop below 15m swing (71.10-71.25), size down to ~0.5-0.7% equity risk, and wait for the 72.20-72.55 fill - no market chase.
transcript.portfolio: Honored the VETO: no add. With existing SOL at ~15% equity (3x the 5% cap) and leaders wobbling into an overbought, rolling-over tape, trim ~10 SOL to bring exposure back toward the mandated 5% limit rather than chase into noise.
